reporte.descripcion: If you find a potential security vulnerability in SolarisPKN Labs, please report it to:
requisitos: a clear description of the vulnerability;
the affected URLs, endpoints, systems, or components;
screenshots, proof of concept, or other relevant information.
reporte.nota: A fully developed proof of concept is not required to report a vulnerability.

solicitudes: minimize the impact of their testing;
avoid accessing information that does not belong to them;
stop testing if there is a risk of affecting other users or services.
noPermitidas: unauthorized physical access to facilities or equipment;
accessing, modifying, or deleting third-party data;
attacks against systems, domains, or users outside the scope.
pruebas.aviso: The existence of this policy does not authorize illegal activities or access to systems outside the defined scope.

divulgacion.descripcion: We encourage reporting vulnerabilities to SolarisPKN before public disclosure.
divulgacion.tiempo: Researchers should allow a reasonable time for the issue to be evaluated and, if possible, mitigated before public disclosure.
divulgacion.evaluacionIntro: SolarisPKN will evaluate each report individually. Response and resolution times will depend on:
evaluacion: the technical complexity of the issue;
the scope of the affected systems.
divulgacion.nota: No fixed resolution timeframe is guaranteed for all reports.
divulgacion.coordinacion: When appropriate, SolarisPKN may coordinate public disclosure with the researcher.

reconocimiento.descripcion: Researchers who report vulnerabilities responsibly may be publicly acknowledged for their contribution, provided that:
condiciones: the disclosure of recognition does not create security or privacy risks.

alcance.descripcion: This policy applies to SolarisPKN Labs services and systems identified within the corresponding scope.
alcance.otras: For reports related to other projects, domains, or associated services, please clearly indicate the affected resource in the message.

contacto.pgp: We recommend encrypting sensitive communications using OpenPGP when an official public key is available.
